1→package checkpoint
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"crypto/sha256"
     6→	"encoding/hex"
     7→	"encoding/json"
     8→	"errors"
     9→	"fmt"
    10→	"log/slog"
    11→	"os"
    12→	"os/exec"
    13→	"path/filepath"
    14→	"sort"
    15→	"strings"
    16→	"time"
    17→
    18→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    19→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/jsonutil"
    20→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    21→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    22→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/trailers"
    23→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/validation"
    24→	"github.com/entireio/cli/redact"
    25→
    26→	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5"
    27→	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
    28→	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing/filemode"
    29→	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing/object"
    30→)
    31→
    32→const (
    33→	// ShadowBranchPrefix is the prefix for shadow branches.
    34→	ShadowBranchPrefix = "entire/"
    35→
    36→	// ShadowBranchHashLength is the number of hex characters used in shadow branch names.
    37→	// Shadow branches are named "entire/<hash>" using the first 7 characters of the commit hash.
    38→	ShadowBranchHashLength = 7
    39→
    40→	// WorktreeIDHashLength is the number of hex characters used for worktree ID hash.
    41→	WorktreeIDHashLength = 6
    42→)
    43→
    44→// HashWorktreeID returns a short hash of the worktree identifier.
    45→// Used to create unique shadow branch names per worktree.
    46→func HashWorktreeID(worktreeID string) string {
    47→	h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(worktreeID))
    48→	return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])[:WorktreeIDHashLength]
    49→}
    50→
    51→// WriteTemporary writes a temporary checkpoint to a shadow branch.
    52→// Shadow branches are named entire/<base-commit-short-hash>.
    53→// Returns the result containing commit hash and whether it was skipped.
    54→// If the new tree hash matches the last checkpoint's tree hash, the checkpoint
    55→// is skipped to avoid duplicate commits (deduplication).
    56→func (s *GitStore) WriteTemporary(ctx context.Context, opts WriteTemporaryOptions) (WriteTemporaryResult, error) {
    57→	// Validate base commit - required for shadow branch naming
    58→	if opts.BaseCommit == "" {
    59→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, errors.New("BaseCommit is required for temporary checkpoint")
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	// Validate session ID to prevent path traversal
    63→	if err := validation.ValidateSessionID(opts.SessionID); err != nil {
    64→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid temporary checkpoint options: %w", err)
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	// Get shadow branch name
    68→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(opts.BaseCommit, opts.WorktreeID)
    69→
    70→	// Get or create shadow branch
    71→	parentHash, baseTreeHash, err := s.getOrCreateShadowBranch(shadowBranchName)
    72→	if err != nil {
    73→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to get shadow branch: %w", err)
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	// Get the last checkpoint's tree hash for deduplication
    77→	var lastTreeHash plumbing.Hash
    78→	if parentHash != plumbing.ZeroHash {
    79→		if lastCommit, err := s.repo.CommitObject(parentHash); err == nil {
    80→			lastTreeHash = lastCommit.TreeHash
    81→		}
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	// Collect all files to include
    85→	var allFiles []string
    86→	var allDeletedFiles []string
    87→	if opts.IsFirstCheckpoint {
    88→		// For the first checkpoint, capture all changed files (modified tracked + untracked)
    89→		// using `git status --porcelain -z` which respects both repo and global .gitignore.
    90→		// This is much faster than filesystem walk. The base tree from HEAD already contains
    91→		// all unchanged tracked files. We also capture user's pre-existing deletions.
    92→		result, err := collectChangedFiles(ctx, s.repo)
    93→		if err != nil {
    94→			return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to collect changed files: %w", err)
    95→		}
    96→		allFiles = result.Changed
    97→		// Merge user's pre-existing deletions with agent's deletions
    98→		allDeletedFiles = result.Deleted
    99→		allDeletedFiles = append(allDeletedFiles, opts.DeletedFiles...)
   100→	} else {
   101→		// For subsequent checkpoints, only include modified/new files
   102→		allFiles = make([]string, 0, len(opts.ModifiedFiles)+len(opts.NewFiles))
   103→		allFiles = append(allFiles, opts.ModifiedFiles...)
   104→		allFiles = append(allFiles, opts.NewFiles...)
   105→		allDeletedFiles = opts.DeletedFiles
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Build tree with changes
   109→	treeHash, err := s.buildTreeWithChanges(baseTreeHash, allFiles, allDeletedFiles, opts.MetadataDir, opts.MetadataDirAbs)
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build tree: %w", err)
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// Deduplication: skip if tree hash matches the last checkpoint
   115→	if lastTreeHash != plumbing.ZeroHash && treeHash == lastTreeHash {
   116→		return WriteTemporaryResult{
   117→			CommitHash: parentHash,
   118→			Skipped:    true,
   119→		}, nil
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// Create checkpoint commit with trailers
   123→	commitMsg := trailers.FormatShadowCommit(opts.CommitMessage, opts.MetadataDir, opts.SessionID)
   124→
   125→	commitHash, err := s.createCommit(treeHash, parentHash, commitMsg, opts.AuthorName, opts.AuthorEmail)
   126→	if err != nil {
   127→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to create commit: %w", err)
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	// Update branch reference
   131→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   132→	newRef := plumbing.NewHashReference(refName, commitHash)
   133→	if err := s.repo.Storer.SetReference(newRef); err != nil {
   134→		return WriteTemporaryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to update branch reference: %w", err)
   135→	}
   136→
   137→	return WriteTemporaryResult{
   138→		CommitHash: commitHash,
   139→		Skipped:    false,
   140→	}, nil
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ReadTemporary reads the latest checkpoint from a shadow branch.
   144→// Returns nil if the shadow branch doesn't exist.
   145→// worktreeID should be empty for main worktree or the internal git worktree name for linked worktrees.
   146→func (s *GitStore) ReadTemporary(ctx context.Context, baseCommit, worktreeID string) (*ReadTemporaryResult, error) {
   147→	_ = ctx // Reserved for future use
   148→
   149→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(baseCommit, worktreeID)
   150→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   151→
   152→	ref, err := s.repo.Reference(refName, true)
   153→	if err != nil {
   154→		return nil, nil //nolint:nilnil,nilerr // Branch not found is an expected case
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	commit, err := s.repo.CommitObject(ref.Hash())
   158→	if err != nil {
   159→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit object: %w", err)
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	// Extract session ID and metadata dir from commit trailers
   163→	sessionID, _ := trailers.ParseSession(commit.Message)
   164→	metadataDir, _ := trailers.ParseMetadata(commit.Message)
   165→
   166→	return &ReadTemporaryResult{
   167→		CommitHash:  ref.Hash(),
   168→		TreeHash:    commit.TreeHash,
   169→		SessionID:   sessionID,
   170→		MetadataDir: metadataDir,
   171→		Timestamp:   commit.Author.When,
   172→	}, nil
   173→}
   174→
   175→// ListTemporary lists all shadow branches with their checkpoint info.
   176→func (s *GitStore) ListTemporary(ctx context.Context) ([]TemporaryInfo, error) {
   177→	_ = ctx // Reserved for future use
   178→
   179→	iter, err := s.repo.Branches()
   180→	if err != nil {
   181→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list branches: %w", err)
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	var results []TemporaryInfo
   185→	err = iter.ForEach(func(ref *plumbing.Reference) error {
   186→		branchName := ref.Name().Short()
   187→		if !strings.HasPrefix(branchName, ShadowBranchPrefix) {
   188→			return nil
   189→		}
   190→
   191→		// Skip the sessions branch
   192→		if branchName == paths.MetadataBranchName {
   193→			return nil
   194→		}
   195→
   196→		commit, commitErr := s.repo.CommitObject(ref.Hash())
   197→		if commitErr != nil {
   198→			//nolint:nilerr // Skip branches we can't read (non-fatal)
   199→			return nil
   200→		}
   201→
   202→		sessionID, _ := trailers.ParseSession(commit.Message)
   203→
   204→		// Extract base commit from branch name (handles new "entire/<commit>-<worktreeHash>" format)
   205→		baseCommit, _, _ := ParseShadowBranchName(branchName)
   206→
   207→		results = append(results, TemporaryInfo{
   208→			BranchName:   branchName,
   209→			BaseCommit:   baseCommit,
   210→			LatestCommit: ref.Hash(),
   211→			SessionID:    sessionID,
   212→			Timestamp:    commit.Author.When,
   213→		})
   214→
   215→		return nil
   216→	})
   217→
   218→	if err != nil {
   219→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to iterate branches: %w", err)
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	return results, nil
   223→}
   224→
   225→// WriteTemporaryTask writes a task checkpoint to a shadow branch.
   226→// Task checkpoints include both code changes and task-specific metadata.
   227→// Returns the commit hash of the created checkpoint.
   228→func (s *GitStore) WriteTemporaryTask(ctx context.Context, opts WriteTemporaryTaskOptions) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   229→	_ = ctx // Reserved for future use
   230→
   231→	// Validate base commit - required for shadow branch naming
   232→	if opts.BaseCommit == "" {
   233→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, errors.New("BaseCommit is required for task checkpoint")
   234→	}
   235→
   236→	// Validate identifiers to prevent path traversal and malformed data
   237→	if err := validation.ValidateSessionID(opts.SessionID); err != nil {
   238→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("invalid task checkpoint options: %w", err)
   239→	}
   240→	if err := validation.ValidateToolUseID(opts.ToolUseID); err != nil {
   241→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("invalid task checkpoint options: %w", err)
   242→	}
   243→	if err := validation.ValidateAgentID(opts.AgentID); err != nil {
   244→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("invalid task checkpoint options: %w", err)
   245→	}
   246→
   247→	// Get shadow branch name
   248→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(opts.BaseCommit, opts.WorktreeID)
   249→
   250→	// Get or create shadow branch
   251→	parentHash, baseTreeHash, err := s.getOrCreateShadowBranch(shadowBranchName)
   252→	if err != nil {
   253→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get shadow branch: %w", err)
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	// Collect all files to include in the commit
   257→	allFiles := make([]string, 0, len(opts.ModifiedFiles)+len(opts.NewFiles))
   258→	allFiles = append(allFiles, opts.ModifiedFiles...)
   259→	allFiles = append(allFiles, opts.NewFiles...)
   260→
   261→	// Build new tree with code changes (no metadata dir yet)
   262→	newTreeHash, err := s.buildTreeWithChanges(baseTreeHash, allFiles, opts.DeletedFiles, "", "")
   263→	if err != nil {
   264→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to build tree: %w", err)
   265→	}
   266→
   267→	// Add task metadata to tree
   268→	newTreeHash, err = s.addTaskMetadataToTree(newTreeHash, opts)
   269→	if err != nil {
   270→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to add task metadata: %w", err)
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	// Create the commit
   274→	commitHash, err := s.createCommit(newTreeHash, parentHash, opts.CommitMessage, opts.AuthorName, opts.AuthorEmail)
   275→	if err != nil {
   276→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to create commit: %w", err)
   277→	}
   278→
   279→	// Update shadow branch reference
   280→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   281→	ref := plumbing.NewHashReference(refName, commitHash)
   282→	if err := s.repo.Storer.SetReference(ref); err != nil {
   283→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to update shadow branch reference: %w", err)
   284→	}
   285→
   286→	return commitHash, nil
   287→}
   288→
   289→// addTaskMetadataToTree adds task checkpoint metadata to a git tree.
   290→// When IsIncremental is true, only adds the incremental checkpoint file.
   291→func (s *GitStore) addTaskMetadataToTree(baseTreeHash plumbing.Hash, opts WriteTemporaryTaskOptions) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   292→	// Get base tree and flatten it
   293→	baseTree, err := s.repo.TreeObject(baseTreeHash)
   294→	if err != nil {
   295→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get base tree: %w", err)
   296→	}
   297→
   298→	entries := make(map[string]object.TreeEntry)
   299→	if err := FlattenTree(s.repo, baseTree, "", entries); err != nil {
   300→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to flatten tree: %w", err)
   301→	}
   302→
   303→	// Compute metadata paths
   304→	sessionMetadataDir := paths.EntireMetadataDir + "/" + opts.SessionID
   305→	taskMetadataDir := sessionMetadataDir + "/tasks/" + opts.ToolUseID
   306→
   307→	if opts.IsIncremental {
   308→		// Incremental checkpoint: only add the checkpoint file
   309→		// Use proper JSON marshaling to handle nil/empty IncrementalData correctly
   310→		var incData []byte
   311→		if opts.IncrementalData != nil {
   312→			incData, err = redact.JSONLBytes(opts.IncrementalData)
   313→			if err != nil {
   314→				return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to redact incremental checkpoint: %w", err)
   315→			}
   316→		}
   317→		incrementalCheckpoint := struct {
   318→			Type      string          `json:"type"`
   319→			ToolUseID string          `json:"tool_use_id"`
   320→			Timestamp time.Time       `json:"timestamp"`
   321→			Data      json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
   322→		}{
   323→			Type:      opts.IncrementalType,
   324→			ToolUseID: opts.ToolUseID,
   325→			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
   326→			Data:      incData,
   327→		}
   328→		cpData, err := jsonutil.MarshalIndentWithNewline(incrementalCheckpoint, "", "  ")
   329→		if err != nil {
   330→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal incremental checkpoint: %w", err)
   331→		}
   332→
   333→		cpBlobHash, err := CreateBlobFromContent(s.repo, cpData)
   334→		if err != nil {
   335→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to create incremental checkpoint blob: %w", err)
   336→		}
   337→		cpFilename := fmt.Sprintf("%03d-%s.json", opts.IncrementalSequence, opts.ToolUseID)
   338→		cpPath := taskMetadataDir + "/checkpoints/" + cpFilename
   339→		entries[cpPath] = object.TreeEntry{
   340→			Name: cpPath,
   341→			Mode: filemode.Regular,
   342→			Hash: cpBlobHash,
   343→		}
   344→	} else {
   345→		// Final checkpoint: add transcripts and checkpoint.json
   346→
   347→		// Add session transcript (with chunking support for large transcripts)
   348→		if opts.TranscriptPath != "" {
   349→			if transcriptContent, readErr := os.ReadFile(opts.TranscriptPath); readErr == nil {
   350→				// Detect agent type from content for proper chunking
   351→				agentType := agent.DetectAgentTypeFromContent(transcriptContent)
   352→
   353→				// Chunk if necessary
   354→				chunks, chunkErr := agent.ChunkTranscript(transcriptContent, agentType)
   355→				if chunkErr != nil {
   356→					logging.Warn(context.Background(), "failed to chunk transcript, checkpoint will be saved without transcript",
   357→						slog.String("error", chunkErr.Error()),
   358→						slog.String("session_id", opts.SessionID),
   359→					)
   360→				} else {
   361→					for i, chunk := range chunks {
   362→						chunkPath := sessionMetadataDir + "/" + agent.ChunkFileName(paths.TranscriptFileName, i)
   363→						blobHash, blobErr := CreateBlobFromContent(s.repo, chunk)
   364→						if blobErr != nil {
   365→							logging.Warn(context.Background(), "failed to create blob for transcript chunk",
   366→								slog.String("error", blobErr.Error()),
   367→								slog.String("session_id", opts.SessionID),
   368→								slog.Int("chunk_index", i),
   369→							)
   370→							continue
   371→						}
   372→						entries[chunkPath] = object.TreeEntry{
   373→							Name: chunkPath,
   374→							Mode: filemode.Regular,
   375→							Hash: blobHash,
   376→						}
   377→					}
   378→				}
   379→			}
   380→		}
   381→
   382→		// Add subagent transcript if available
   383→		if opts.SubagentTranscriptPath != "" && opts.AgentID != "" {
   384→			if agentContent, readErr := os.ReadFile(opts.SubagentTranscriptPath); readErr == nil {
   385→				if blobHash, blobErr := CreateBlobFromContent(s.repo, agentContent); blobErr == nil {
   386→					agentPath := taskMetadataDir + "/agent-" + opts.AgentID + ".jsonl"
   387→					entries[agentPath] = object.TreeEntry{
   388→						Name: agentPath,
   389→						Mode: filemode.Regular,
   390→						Hash: blobHash,
   391→					}
   392→				}
   393→			}
   394→		}
   395→
   396→		// Add checkpoint.json
   397→		checkpointJSON := fmt.Sprintf(`{
   398→  "session_id": %q,
   399→  "tool_use_id": %q,
   400→  "checkpoint_uuid": %q,
   401→  "agent_id": %q
   402→}`, opts.SessionID, opts.ToolUseID, opts.CheckpointUUID, opts.AgentID)
   403→
   404→		blobHash, err := CreateBlobFromContent(s.repo, []byte(checkpointJSON))
   405→		if err != nil {
   406→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to create checkpoint blob: %w", err)
   407→		}
   408→		checkpointPath := taskMetadataDir + "/checkpoint.json"
   409→		entries[checkpointPath] = object.TreeEntry{
   410→			Name: checkpointPath,
   411→			Mode: filemode.Regular,
   412→			Hash: blobHash,
   413→		}
   414→	}
   415→
   416→	// Build new tree from entries
   417→	return BuildTreeFromEntries(s.repo, entries)
   418→}
   419→
   420→// ListTemporaryCheckpoints lists all checkpoint commits on a shadow branch.
   421→// This returns individual commits (rewind points), not just branch info.
   422→// The sessionID filter, if provided, limits results to commits from that session.
   423→// worktreeID should be empty for main worktree or the internal git worktree name for linked worktrees.
   424→func (s *GitStore) ListTemporaryCheckpoints(ctx context.Context, baseCommit, worktreeID, sessionID string, limit int) ([]TemporaryCheckpointInfo, error) {
   425→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(baseCommit, worktreeID)
   426→	return s.listCheckpointsForBranch(ctx, shadowBranchName, sessionID, limit)
   427→}
   428→
   429→// ListCheckpointsForBranch lists checkpoint commits for a shadow branch by name.
   430→// Use this when you already have the full branch name (e.g., from ListTemporary).
   431→// The sessionID filter, if provided, limits results to commits from that session.
   432→func (s *GitStore) ListCheckpointsForBranch(ctx context.Context, branchName, sessionID string, limit int) ([]TemporaryCheckpointInfo, error) {
   433→	return s.listCheckpointsForBranch(ctx, branchName, sessionID, limit)
   434→}
   435→
   436→// listCheckpointsForBranch lists checkpoint commits for a specific shadow branch name.
   437→// This is an internal helper used by ListTemporaryCheckpoints, ListCheckpointsForBranch, and ListAllTemporaryCheckpoints.
   438→func (s *GitStore) listCheckpointsForBranch(ctx context.Context, shadowBranchName, sessionID string, limit int) ([]TemporaryCheckpointInfo, error) {
   439→	_ = ctx // Reserved for future use
   440→
   441→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   442→
   443→	ref, err := s.repo.Reference(refName, true)
   444→	if err != nil {
   445→		return nil, nil //nolint:nilerr // No shadow branch is expected case
   446→	}
   447→
   448→	iter, err := s.repo.Log(&git.LogOptions{From: ref.Hash()})
   449→	if err != nil {
   450→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit log: %w", err)
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	var results []TemporaryCheckpointInfo
   454→	count := 0
   455→
   456→	err = iter.ForEach(func(c *object.Commit) error {
   457→		if count >= limit*5 { // Scan more to allow for session filtering
   458→			return errStop
   459→		}
   460→		count++
   461→
   462→		// Verify commit belongs to target session via Entire-Session trailer
   463→		commitSessionID, hasTrailer := trailers.ParseSession(c.Message)
   464→		if !hasTrailer {
   465→			return nil // Skip commits without session trailer
   466→		}
   467→		if sessionID != "" && commitSessionID != sessionID {
   468→			return nil // Skip commits from other sessions
   469→		}
   470→
   471→		// Get first line of message
   472→		message := c.Message
   473→		if idx := strings.Index(message, "\n"); idx > 0 {
   474→			message = message[:idx]
   475→		}
   476→
   477→		info := TemporaryCheckpointInfo{
   478→			CommitHash: c.Hash,
   479→			Message:    message,
   480→			SessionID:  commitSessionID,
   481→			Timestamp:  c.Author.When,
   482→		}
   483→
   484→		// Check for task checkpoint first
   485→		taskMetadataDir, foundTask := trailers.ParseTaskMetadata(c.Message)
   486→		if foundTask {
   487→			info.IsTaskCheckpoint = true
   488→			info.MetadataDir = taskMetadataDir
   489→			info.ToolUseID = extractToolUseIDFromPath(taskMetadataDir)
   490→		} else {
   491→			metadataDir, found := trailers.ParseMetadata(c.Message)
   492→			if found {
   493→				info.MetadataDir = metadataDir
   494→			}
   495→		}
   496→
   497→		results = append(results, info)
   498→
   499→		if len(results) >= limit {
   500→			return errStop
   501→		}
   502→		return nil
   503→	})
   504→
   505→	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, errStop) {
   506→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error iterating commits: %w", err)
   507→	}
   508→
   509→	return results, nil
   510→}
   511→
   512→// ListAllTemporaryCheckpoints lists checkpoint commits from ALL shadow branches.
   513→// This is used for checkpoint lookup when the base commit is unknown (e.g., HEAD advanced since session start).
   514→// The sessionID filter, if provided, limits results to commits from that session.
   515→func (s *GitStore) ListAllTemporaryCheckpoints(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, limit int) ([]TemporaryCheckpointInfo, error) {
   516→	_ = ctx // Reserved for future use
   517→
   518→	// List all shadow branches
   519→	branches, err := s.ListTemporary(ctx)
   520→	if err != nil {
   521→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list shadow branches: %w", err)
   522→	}
   523→
   524→	var results []TemporaryCheckpointInfo
   525→
   526→	// Iterate through each shadow branch and collect checkpoints
   527→	for _, branch := range branches {
   528→		// Use the branch name directly to get checkpoints
   529→		branchCheckpoints, branchErr := s.listCheckpointsForBranch(ctx, branch.BranchName, sessionID, limit)
   530→		if branchErr != nil {
   531→			continue // Skip branches we can't read
   532→		}
   533→		results = append(results, branchCheckpoints...)
   534→		if len(results) >= limit {
   535→			results = results[:limit]
   536→			break
   537→		}
   538→	}
   539→
   540→	return results, nil
   541→}
   542→
   543→// extractToolUseIDFromPath extracts the ToolUseID from a task metadata directory path.
   544→// Task metadata dirs have format: .entire/metadata/<session>/tasks/<toolUseID>
   545→func extractToolUseIDFromPath(metadataDir string) string {
   546→	parts := strings.Split(metadataDir, "/")
   547→	if len(parts) >= 2 && parts[len(parts)-2] == "tasks" {
   548→		return parts[len(parts)-1]
   549→	}
   550→	return ""
   551→}
   552→
   553→// errStop is a sentinel error used to break out of git log iteration.
   554→var errStop = errors.New("stop iteration")
   555→
   556→// GetTranscriptFromCommit retrieves the transcript from a specific commit's tree.
   557→// This is used for shadow branch checkpoints where the transcript is stored in the commit tree
   558→// rather than on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch.
   559→// commitHash is the commit to read from, metadataDir is the path within the tree.
   560→// agentType is used for reassembling chunked transcripts in the correct format.
   561→// Handles both chunked and non-chunked transcripts.
   562→func (s *GitStore) GetTranscriptFromCommit(commitHash plumbing.Hash, metadataDir string, agentType agent.AgentType) ([]byte, error) {
   563→	commit, err := s.repo.CommitObject(commitHash)
   564→	if err != nil {
   565→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit: %w", err)
   566→	}
   567→
   568→	tree, err := commit.Tree()
   569→	if err != nil {
   570→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit tree: %w", err)
   571→	}
   572→
   573→	// Try to get the metadata subtree for chunk detection
   574→	subTree, subTreeErr := tree.Tree(metadataDir)
   575→	if subTreeErr == nil {
   576→		// Use the helper function that handles chunking
   577→		transcript, err := readTranscriptFromTree(subTree, agentType)
   578→		if err == nil && transcript != nil {
   579→			return transcript, nil
   580→		}
   581→	}
   582→
   583→	// Fall back to direct file access (for backwards compatibility)
   584→	transcriptPath := metadataDir + "/" + paths.TranscriptFileName
   585→	if file, fileErr := tree.File(transcriptPath); fileErr == nil {
   586→		content, contentErr := file.Contents()
   587→		if contentErr == nil {
   588→			return []byte(content), nil
   589→		}
   590→	}
   591→
   592→	transcriptPath = metadataDir + "/" + paths.TranscriptFileNameLegacy
   593→	if file, fileErr := tree.File(transcriptPath); fileErr == nil {
   594→		content, contentErr := file.Contents()
   595→		if contentErr == nil {
   596→			return []byte(content), nil
   597→		}
   598→	}
   599→
   600→	return nil, ErrNoTranscript
   601→}
   602→
   603→// ShadowBranchExists checks if a shadow branch exists for the given base commit and worktree.
   604→// worktreeID should be empty for main worktree or the internal git worktree name for linked worktrees.
   605→func (s *GitStore) ShadowBranchExists(baseCommit, worktreeID string) bool {
   606→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(baseCommit, worktreeID)
   607→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   608→	_, err := s.repo.Reference(refName, true)
   609→	return err == nil
   610→}
   611→
   612→// DeleteShadowBranch deletes the shadow branch for the given base commit and worktree.
   613→// worktreeID should be empty for main worktree or the internal git worktree name for linked worktrees.
   614→// Uses git CLI instead of go-git's RemoveReference because go-git v5 doesn't properly
   615→// persist deletions with packed refs or worktrees.
   616→func (s *GitStore) DeleteShadowBranch(baseCommit, worktreeID string) error {
   617→	shadowBranchName := ShadowBranchNameForCommit(baseCommit, worktreeID)
   618→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(context.Background(), "git", "branch", "-D", "--", shadowBranchName) //nolint:gosec // shadowBranchName is constructed from commit hash, not user input
   619→	if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
   620→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to delete shadow branch %s: %s: %w", shadowBranchName, strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), err)
   621→	}
   622→	return nil
   623→}
   624→
   625→// ShadowBranchNameForCommit returns the shadow branch name for a base commit hash
   626→// and worktree identifier. The worktree ID should be empty for the main worktree
   627→// or the internal git worktree name for linked worktrees.
   628→// Format: entire/<commit[:7]>-<hash(worktreeID)[:6]>
   629→func ShadowBranchNameForCommit(baseCommit, worktreeID string) string {
   630→	commitPart := baseCommit
   631→	if len(baseCommit) >= ShadowBranchHashLength {
   632→		commitPart = baseCommit[:ShadowBranchHashLength]
   633→	}
   634→	worktreeHash := HashWorktreeID(worktreeID)
   635→	return ShadowBranchPrefix + commitPart + "-" + worktreeHash
   636→}
   637→
   638→// ParseShadowBranchName extracts the commit prefix and worktree hash from a shadow branch name.
   639→// Input format: "entire/<commit[:7]>-<worktreeHash[:6]>"
   640→// Returns (commitPrefix, worktreeHash, ok). Returns ("", "", false) if not a valid shadow branch.
   641→func ParseShadowBranchName(branchName string) (commitPrefix, worktreeHash string, ok bool) {
   642→	if !strings.HasPrefix(branchName, ShadowBranchPrefix) {
   643→		return "", "", false
   644→	}
   645→	suffix := strings.TrimPrefix(branchName, ShadowBranchPrefix)
   646→
   647→	// Find the last dash - everything before is commit prefix, after is worktree hash
   648→	lastDash := strings.LastIndex(suffix, "-")
   649→	if lastDash == -1 || lastDash == 0 || lastDash == len(suffix)-1 {
   650→		// No dash, or dash at start/end - invalid format
   651→		// Could be old format "entire/<commit[:7]>" without worktree hash
   652→		return suffix, "", true // Return as commit prefix with empty worktree hash
   653→	}
   654→
   655→	return suffix[:lastDash], suffix[lastDash+1:], true
   656→}
   657→
   658→// getOrCreateShadowBranch gets or creates the shadow branch for checkpoints.
   659→// Returns (parentHash, baseTreeHash, error).
   660→func (s *GitStore) getOrCreateShadowBranch(branchName string) (plumbing.Hash, plumbing.Hash, error) {
   661→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(branchName)
   662→	ref, err := s.repo.Reference(refName, true)
   663→
   664→	if err == nil {
   665→		// Branch exists
   666→		commit, err := s.repo.CommitObject(ref.Hash())
   667→		if err != nil {
   668→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit object: %w", err)
   669→		}
   670→		return ref.Hash(), commit.TreeHash, nil
   671→	}
   672→
   673→	// Branch doesn't exist, use current HEAD's tree as base
   674→	head, err := s.repo.Head()
   675→	if err != nil {
   676→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD: %w", err)
   677→	}
   678→
   679→	headCommit, err := s.repo.CommitObject(head.Hash())
   680→	if err != nil {
   681→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD commit: %w", err)
   682→	}
   683→
   684→	return plumbing.ZeroHash, headCommit.TreeHash, nil
   685→}
   686→
   687→// buildTreeWithChanges builds a git tree with the given changes.
   688→// metadataDir is the relative path for git tree entries, metadataDirAbs is the absolute path
   689→// for filesystem operations (needed when CLI is run from a subdirectory).
   690→func (s *GitStore) buildTreeWithChanges(
   691→	baseTreeHash plumbing.Hash,
   692→	modifiedFiles, deletedFiles []string,
   693→	metadataDir, metadataDirAbs string,
   694→) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   695→	// Get repo root for resolving file paths
   696→	// This is critical because fileExists() and createBlobFromFile() use os.Stat()
   697→	// which resolves relative to CWD. The modifiedFiles are repo-relative paths,
   698→	// so we must resolve them against repo root, not CWD.
   699→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   700→	if err != nil {
   701→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   702→	}
   703→
   704→	// Get the base tree
   705→	baseTree, err := s.repo.TreeObject(baseTreeHash)
   706→	if err != nil {
   707→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to get base tree: %w", err)
   708→	}
   709→
   710→	// Flatten existing tree
   711→	entries := make(map[string]object.TreeEntry)
   712→	if err := FlattenTree(s.repo, baseTree, "", entries); err != nil {
   713→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to flatten base tree: %w", err)
   714→	}
   715→
   716→	// Remove deleted files
   717→	for _, file := range deletedFiles {
   718→		delete(entries, file)
   719→	}
   720→
   721→	// Add/update modified files
   722→	for _, file := range modifiedFiles {
   723→		// Resolve path relative to repo root for filesystem operations
   724→		absPath := filepath.Join(repoRoot, file)
   725→		if !fileExists(absPath) {
   726→			delete(entries, file)
   727→			continue
   728→		}
   729→
   730→		blobHash, mode, err := createBlobFromFile(s.repo, absPath)
   731→		if err != nil {
   732→			// Skip files that can't be staged (may have been deleted since detection)
   733→			continue
   734→		}
   735→
   736→		entries[file] = object.TreeEntry{
   737→			Name: file,
   738→			Mode: mode,
   739→			Hash: blobHash,
   740→		}
   741→	}
   742→
   743→	// Add metadata directory files
   744→	if metadataDir != "" && metadataDirAbs != "" {
   745→		if err := addDirectoryToEntriesWithAbsPath(s.repo, metadataDirAbs, metadataDir, entries); err != nil {
   746→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to add metadata directory: %w", err)
   747→		}
   748→	}
   749→
   750→	// Build tree
   751→	return BuildTreeFromEntries(s.repo, entries)
   752→}
   753→
   754→// createCommit creates a commit object.
   755→func (s *GitStore) createCommit(treeHash, parentHash plumbing.Hash, message, authorName, authorEmail string) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   756→	now := time.Now()
   757→	sig := object.Signature{
   758→		Name:  authorName,
   759→		Email: authorEmail,
   760→		When:  now,
   761→	}
   762→
   763→	commit := &object.Commit{
   764→		TreeHash:  treeHash,
   765→		Author:    sig,
   766→		Committer: sig,
   767→		Message:   message,
   768→	}
   769→
   770→	// Add parent if not a new branch
   771→	if parentHash != plumbing.ZeroHash {
   772→		commit.ParentHashes = []plumbing.Hash{parentHash}
   773→	}
   774→
   775→	obj := s.repo.Storer.NewEncodedObject()
   776→	if err := commit.Encode(obj); err != nil {
   777→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode commit: %w", err)
   778→	}
   779→
   780→	hash, err := s.repo.Storer.SetEncodedObject(obj)
   781→	if err != nil {
   782→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to store commit: %w", err)
   783→	}
   784→
   785→	return hash, nil
   786→}
   787→
   788→// Helper functions extracted from strategy/common.go
   789→// These are exported for use by strategy package (push_common.go, session_test.go)
   790→
   791→// FlattenTree recursively flattens a tree into a map of full paths to entries.
   792→func FlattenTree(repo *git.Repository, tree *object.Tree, prefix string, entries map[string]object.TreeEntry) error {
   793→	for _, entry := range tree.Entries {
   794→		fullPath := entry.Name
   795→		if prefix != "" {
   796→			fullPath = prefix + "/" + entry.Name
   797→		}
   798→
   799→		if entry.Mode == filemode.Dir {
   800→			// Recurse into subtree
   801→			subtree, err := repo.TreeObject(entry.Hash)
   802→			if err != nil {
   803→				return fmt.Errorf("failed to get subtree %s: %w", fullPath, err)
   804→			}
   805→			if err := FlattenTree(repo, subtree, fullPath, entries); err != nil {
   806→				return err
   807→			}
   808→		} else {
   809→			entries[fullPath] = object.TreeEntry{
   810→				Name: fullPath,
   811→				Mode: entry.Mode,
   812→				Hash: entry.Hash,
   813→			}
   814→		}
   815→	}
   816→	return nil
   817→}
   818→
   819→// fileExists checks if a file exists at the given path.
   820→func fileExists(path string) bool {
   821→	_, err := os.Stat(path)
   822→	return err == nil
   823→}
   824→
   825→// createBlobFromFile creates a blob object from a file in the working directory.
   826→func createBlobFromFile(repo *git.Repository, filePath string) (plumbing.Hash, filemode.FileMode, error) {
   827→	info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
   828→	if err != nil {
   829→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to stat file: %w", err)
   830→	}
   831→
   832→	// Determine file mode
   833→	mode := filemode.Regular
   834→	if info.Mode()&0o111 != 0 {
   835→		mode = filemode.Executable
   836→	}
   837→	if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
   838→		mode = filemode.Symlink
   839→	}
   840→
   841→	// Read file contents
   842→	content, err := os.ReadFile(filePath) //nolint:gosec // filePath comes from walking the repository
   843→	if err != nil {
   844→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to read file: %w", err)
   845→	}
   846→
   847→	// Create blob object
   848→	obj := repo.Storer.NewEncodedObject()
   849→	obj.SetType(plumbing.BlobObject)
   850→	obj.SetSize(int64(len(content)))
   851→
   852→	writer, err := obj.Writer()
   853→	if err != nil {
   854→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to get object writer: %w", err)
   855→	}
   856→
   857→	_, err = writer.Write(content)
   858→	if err != nil {
   859→		_ = writer.Close()
   860→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to write blob content: %w", err)
   861→	}
   862→	if err := writer.Close(); err != nil {
   863→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to close blob writer: %w", err)
   864→	}
   865→
   866→	hash, err := repo.Storer.SetEncodedObject(obj)
   867→	if err != nil {
   868→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to store blob object: %w", err)
   869→	}
   870→
   871→	return hash, mode, nil
   872→}
   873→
   874→// addDirectoryToEntriesWithAbsPath recursively adds all files in a directory to the entries map.
   875→func addDirectoryToEntriesWithAbsPath(repo *git.Repository, dirPathAbs, dirPathRel string, entries map[string]object.TreeEntry) error {
   876→	err := filepath.Walk(dirPathAbs, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
   877→		if err != nil {
   878→			return err
   879→		}
   880→		if info.IsDir() {
   881→			return nil
   882→		}
   883→
   884→		// Calculate relative path within the directory, then join with dirPathRel for tree entry
   885→		relWithinDir, err := filepath.Rel(dirPathAbs, path)
   886→		if err != nil {
   887→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get relative path for %s: %w", path, err)
   888→		}
   889→
   890→		blobHash, mode, err := createBlobFromFile(repo, path)
   891→		if err != nil {
   892→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create blob for %s: %w", path, err)
   893→		}
   894→
   895→		treePath := filepath.Join(dirPathRel, relWithinDir)
   896→		entries[treePath] = object.TreeEntry{
   897→			Name: treePath,
   898→			Mode: mode,
   899→			Hash: blobHash,
   900→		}
   901→		return nil
   902→	})
   903→	if err != nil {
   904→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to walk directory %s: %w", dirPathAbs, err)
   905→	}
   906→	return nil
   907→}
   908→
   909→// treeNode represents a node in our tree structure.
   910→type treeNode struct {
   911→	entries map[string]*treeNode // subdirectories
   912→	files   []object.TreeEntry   // files in this directory
   913→}
   914→
   915→// BuildTreeFromEntries builds a proper git tree structure from flattened file entries.
   916→// Exported for use by strategy package (push_common.go, session_test.go)
   917→func BuildTreeFromEntries(repo *git.Repository, entries map[string]object.TreeEntry) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   918→	// Build a tree structure
   919→	root := &treeNode{
   920→		entries: make(map[string]*treeNode),
   921→		files:   []object.TreeEntry{},
   922→	}
   923→
   924→	// Insert all entries into the tree structure
   925→	for fullPath, entry := range entries {
   926→		parts := strings.Split(fullPath, "/")
   927→		insertIntoTree(root, parts, entry)
   928→	}
   929→
   930→	// Recursively build tree objects from bottom up
   931→	return buildTreeObject(repo, root)
   932→}
   933→
   934→// insertIntoTree inserts a file entry into the tree structure.
   935→func insertIntoTree(node *treeNode, pathParts []string, entry object.TreeEntry) {
   936→	if len(pathParts) == 1 {
   937→		// This is a file in the current directory
   938→		node.files = append(node.files, object.TreeEntry{
   939→			Name: pathParts[0],
   940→			Mode: entry.Mode,
   941→			Hash: entry.Hash,
   942→		})
   943→		return
   944→	}
   945→
   946→	// This is in a subdirectory
   947→	dirName := pathParts[0]
   948→	if node.entries[dirName] == nil {
   949→		node.entries[dirName] = &treeNode{
   950→			entries: make(map[string]*treeNode),
   951→			files:   []object.TreeEntry{},
   952→		}
   953→	}
   954→	insertIntoTree(node.entries[dirName], pathParts[1:], entry)
   955→}
   956→
   957→// buildTreeObject recursively builds tree objects from a treeNode.
   958→func buildTreeObject(repo *git.Repository, node *treeNode) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
   959→	var treeEntries []object.TreeEntry
   960→
   961→	// Add files
   962→	treeEntries = append(treeEntries, node.files...)
   963→
   964→	// Recursively build subtrees
   965→	for name, subnode := range node.entries {
   966→		subHash, err := buildTreeObject(repo, subnode)
   967→		if err != nil {
   968→			return plumbing.ZeroHash, err
   969→		}
   970→		treeEntries = append(treeEntries, object.TreeEntry{
   971→			Name: name,
   972→			Mode: filemode.Dir,
   973→			Hash: subHash,
   974→		})
   975→	}
   976→
   977→	// Sort entries (git requires sorted entries)
   978→	sortTreeEntries(treeEntries)
   979→
   980→	// Create tree object
   981→	tree := &object.Tree{Entries: treeEntries}
   982→
   983→	obj := repo.Storer.NewEncodedObject()
   984→	if err := tree.Encode(obj); err != nil {
   985→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode tree: %w", err)
   986→	}
   987→
   988→	hash, err := repo.Storer.SetEncodedObject(obj)
   989→	if err != nil {
   990→		return plumbing.ZeroHash, fmt.Errorf("failed to store tree: %w", err)
   991→	}
   992→
   993→	return hash, nil
   994→}
   995→
   996→// sortTreeEntries sorts tree entries in git's required order.
   997→// Git sorts tree entries by name, with directories having a trailing /
   998→func sortTreeEntries(entries []object.TreeEntry) {
   999→	sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool {
  1000→		nameI := entries[i].Name
  1001→		nameJ := entries[j].Name
  1002→		if entries[i].Mode == filemode.Dir {
  1003→			nameI += "/"
  1004→		}
  1005→		if entries[j].Mode == filemode.Dir {
  1006→			nameJ += "/"
  1007→		}
  1008→		return nameI < nameJ
  1009→	})
  1010→}
  1011→
  1012→// collectChangedFiles collects all changed files (modified tracked + untracked non-ignored)
  1013→// using git CLI. This is much faster than filesystem walk and respects all gitignore sources
  1014→// including global gitignore (core.excludesfile).
  1015→//
  1016→// Uses git CLI instead of go-git because go-git's worktree.Status() does not respect
  1017→// global gitignore, which can cause globally ignored files to appear as untracked.
  1018→// See: https://github.com/entireio/cli/pull/129
  1019→//
  1020→// changedFilesResult contains both changed and deleted files from git status.
  1021→type changedFilesResult struct {
  1022→	Changed []string // Files to include (modified, added, untracked, renamed, etc.)
  1023→	Deleted []string // Files that were deleted (need to be excluded from checkpoint tree)
  1024→}
  1025→
  1026→// collectChangedFiles returns all changed files from git status for the first checkpoint.
  1027→//
  1028→// For the first checkpoint, we need to capture:
  1029→// - Modified tracked files (user's uncommitted changes)
  1030→// - Untracked non-ignored files (new files not yet added to git)
  1031→// - Renamed/copied files (both source removal and destination)
  1032→// - Deleted files (to exclude from checkpoint tree)
  1033→//
  1034→// The base tree from HEAD already contains all unchanged tracked files.
  1035→//
  1036→// Uses `git status --porcelain -z` for reliable parsing of filenames with special characters.
  1037→func collectChangedFiles(ctx context.Context, repo *git.Repository) (changedFilesResult, error) {
  1038→	// Get repo root directory for running git command
  1039→	wt, err := repo.Worktree()
  1040→	if err != nil {
  1041→		return changedFilesResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to get worktree: %w", err)
  1042→	}
  1043→	repoRoot := wt.Filesystem.Root()
  1044→
  1045→	// Use -z for NUL-separated output (handles quoted filenames with spaces/special chars)
  1046→	// Use -uall to list individual untracked files instead of collapsed directories.
  1047→	// Note: CLAUDE.md warns against -uall for user-facing display, but we need the full list
  1048→	// for checkpointing.
  1049→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "status", "--porcelain", "-z", "-uall")
  1050→	cmd.Dir = repoRoot
  1051→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  1052→	if err != nil {
  1053→		return changedFilesResult{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to get git status in %s: %w", repoRoot, err)
  1054→	}
  1055→
  1056→	changedSeen := make(map[string]struct{})
  1057→	deletedSeen := make(map[string]struct{})
  1058→
  1059→	// Parse NUL-separated output
  1060→	// Format: XY filename\0 (for most entries)
  1061→	// For renames/copies: XY newname\0oldname\0
  1062→	entries := strings.Split(string(output), "\x00")
  1063→
  1064→	for i := 0; i < len(entries); i++ {
  1065→		entry := entries[i]
  1066→		if len(entry) < 3 {
  1067→			continue
  1068→		}
  1069→
  1070→		// git status --porcelain format: XY filename
  1071→		// X = staging status, Y = worktree status
  1072→		staging := entry[0]
  1073→		wtStatus := entry[1]
  1074→		filename := entry[3:] // No TrimSpace needed with -z format
  1075→
  1076→		// Handle R/C (rename/copy) first - they have a second entry we must skip
  1077→		// even if the new filename is an infrastructure path
  1078→		if staging == 'R' || staging == 'C' {
  1079→			// Renamed or copied: current entry is new name, next entry is old name
  1080→			if !paths.IsInfrastructurePath(filename) {
  1081→				changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1082→			}
  1083→			// The old name follows as the next NUL-separated entry - must always skip it
  1084→			if i+1 < len(entries) && entries[i+1] != "" {
  1085→				oldName := entries[i+1]
  1086→				if staging == 'R' && !paths.IsInfrastructurePath(oldName) {
  1087→					// For renames, old file is effectively deleted
  1088→					deletedSeen[oldName] = struct{}{}
  1089→				}
  1090→				i++ // Skip the old name entry
  1091→			}
  1092→			continue
  1093→		}
  1094→
  1095→		// Skip .entire directory for non-R/C entries
  1096→		if paths.IsInfrastructurePath(filename) {
  1097→			continue
  1098→		}
  1099→
  1100→		// Handle different status codes
  1101→		switch {
  1102→		case staging == 'D' || wtStatus == 'D':
  1103→			// Deleted file - track separately
  1104→			deletedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1105→
  1106→		case wtStatus == 'M' || wtStatus == 'A':
  1107→			// Modified or added in worktree
  1108→			changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1109→
  1110→		case staging == '?' && wtStatus == '?':
  1111→			// Untracked file
  1112→			changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1113→
  1114→		case staging == 'A' || staging == 'M':
  1115→			// Staged add or modify
  1116→			changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1117→
  1118→		case staging == 'T' || wtStatus == 'T':
  1119→			// Type change (e.g., file to symlink)
  1120→			changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1121→
  1122→		case staging == 'U' || wtStatus == 'U':
  1123→			// Unmerged (conflict) - include current file state
  1124→			changedSeen[filename] = struct{}{}
  1125→		}
  1126→	}
  1127→
  1128→	changed := make([]string, 0, len(changedSeen))
  1129→	for file := range changedSeen {
  1130→		changed = append(changed, file)
  1131→	}
  1132→
  1133→	deleted := make([]string, 0, len(deletedSeen))
  1134→	for file := range deletedSeen {
  1135→		deleted = append(deleted, file)
  1136→	}
  1137→
  1138→	return changedFilesResult{Changed: changed, Deleted: deleted}, nil
  1139→}
  1140→

1→package strategy
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"slices"
     5→	"strings"
     6→	"time"
     7→
     8→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/checkpoint"
     9→	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing/object"
    10→	"github.com/sergi/go-diff/diffmatchpatch"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// getAllChangedFilesBetweenTrees returns a list of all files that differ between two trees.
    14→// This includes files that were added, modified, or deleted in either tree.
    15→// Uses git blob hashes for efficient comparison without reading file contents.
    16→func getAllChangedFilesBetweenTrees(tree1, tree2 *object.Tree) []string {
    17→	if tree1 == nil && tree2 == nil {
    18→		return nil
    19→	}
    20→
    21→	// Build hash maps for each tree - O(n) iteration, no content reading
    22→	tree1Hashes := make(map[string]string)
    23→	tree2Hashes := make(map[string]string)
    24→
    25→	if tree1 != nil {
    26→		//nolint:errcheck // Errors ignored - just collecting file hashes for diff comparison
    27→		_ = tree1.Files().ForEach(func(f *object.File) error {
    28→			tree1Hashes[f.Name] = f.Hash.String()
    29→			return nil
    30→		})
    31→	}
    32→
    33→	if tree2 != nil {
    34→		//nolint:errcheck // Errors ignored - just collecting file hashes for diff comparison
    35→		_ = tree2.Files().ForEach(func(f *object.File) error {
    36→			tree2Hashes[f.Name] = f.Hash.String()
    37→			return nil
    38→		})
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	// Find changed files by comparing hashes (much faster than content comparison)
    42→	var changed []string
    43→
    44→	// Check files in tree1 - either modified or deleted in tree2
    45→	for path, hash1 := range tree1Hashes {
    46→		if hash2, exists := tree2Hashes[path]; !exists || hash1 != hash2 {
    47→			changed = append(changed, path)
    48→		}
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	// Check files only in tree2 (added files)
    52→	for path := range tree2Hashes {
    53→		if _, exists := tree1Hashes[path]; !exists {
    54→			changed = append(changed, path)
    55→		}
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	return changed
    59→}
    60→
    61→// getFileContent retrieves the content of a file from a tree.
    62→// Returns empty string if the file doesn't exist, can't be read, or is a binary file.
    63→//
    64→// Binary files are silently excluded from attribution calculations because line-based
    65→// diffing doesn't apply to binary content. This means binary files (images, compiled
    66→// binaries, etc.) won't appear in attribution metrics even if they were added or modified.
    67→// This is intentional - attribution measures code contributions via line counting,
    68→// which only makes sense for text files.
    69→//
    70→// Uses go-git's IsBinary() which implements git's binary detection algorithm.
    71→//
    72→// TODO: Consider tracking binary file counts separately (e.g., BinaryFilesChanged field)
    73→// to provide visibility into non-text file modifications.
    74→func getFileContent(tree *object.Tree, path string) string {
    75→	if tree == nil {
    76→		return ""
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	file, err := tree.File(path)
    80→	if err != nil {
    81→		return ""
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	// Use git's binary detection algorithm
    85→	isBinary, err := file.IsBinary()
    86→	if err != nil || isBinary {
    87→		return ""
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	content, err := file.Contents()
    91→	if err != nil {
    92→		return ""
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	return content
    96→}
    97→
    98→// diffLines compares two strings and returns line-level diff stats.
    99→// Returns (unchanged, added, removed) line counts.
   100→func diffLines(checkpointContent, committedContent string) (unchanged, added, removed int) {
   101→	// Handle edge cases
   102→	if checkpointContent == committedContent {
   103→		return countLinesStr(committedContent), 0, 0
   104→	}
   105→	if checkpointContent == "" {
   106→		return 0, countLinesStr(committedContent), 0
   107→	}
   108→	if committedContent == "" {
   109→		return 0, 0, countLinesStr(checkpointContent)
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	dmp := diffmatchpatch.New()
   113→
   114→	// Convert to line-based diff using DiffLinesToChars/DiffCharsToLines pattern
   115→	text1, text2, lineArray := dmp.DiffLinesToChars(checkpointContent, committedContent)
   116→	diffs := dmp.DiffMain(text1, text2, false)
   117→	diffs = dmp.DiffCharsToLines(diffs, lineArray)
   118→
   119→	for _, d := range diffs {
   120→		lines := countLinesStr(d.Text)
   121→		switch d.Type {
   122→		case diffmatchpatch.DiffEqual:
   123→			unchanged += lines
   124→		case diffmatchpatch.DiffInsert:
   125→			added += lines
   126→		case diffmatchpatch.DiffDelete:
   127→			removed += lines
   128→		}
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	return unchanged, added, removed
   132→}
   133→
   134→// countLinesStr returns the number of lines in a string.
   135→// An empty string has 0 lines. A string without newlines has 1 line.
   136→// This is used for both file content and diff text segments.
   137→func countLinesStr(content string) int {
   138→	if content == "" {
   139→		return 0
   140→	}
   141→	lines := strings.Count(content, "\n")
   142→	// If content doesn't end with newline, add 1 for the last line
   143→	if !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n") {
   144→		lines++
   145→	}
   146→	return lines
   147→}
   148→
   149→// CalculateAttributionWithAccumulated computes final attribution using accumulated prompt data.
   150→// This provides more accurate attribution than tree-only comparison because it captures
   151→// user edits that happened between checkpoints (which would otherwise be mixed into the
   152→// checkpoint snapshots).
   153→//
   154→// The calculation:
   155→// 1. Sum user edits from PromptAttributions (captured at each prompt start)
   156→// 2. Add user edits after the final checkpoint (shadow → head diff)
   157→// 3. Calculate agent lines from base → shadow
   158→// 4. Estimate user self-modifications vs agent modifications using per-file tracking
   159→// 5. Compute percentages
   160→//
   161→// Note: Binary files (detected by null bytes) are silently excluded from attribution
   162→// calculations since line-based diffing only applies to text files.
   163→//
   164→// See docs/architecture/attribution.md for details on the per-file tracking approach.
   165→func CalculateAttributionWithAccumulated(
   166→	baseTree *object.Tree,
   167→	shadowTree *object.Tree,
   168→	headTree *object.Tree,
   169→	filesTouched []string,
   170→	promptAttributions []PromptAttribution,
   171→) *checkpoint.InitialAttribution {
   172→	if len(filesTouched) == 0 {
   173→		return nil
   174→	}
   175→
   176→	// Sum accumulated user lines from prompt attributions
   177→	// Also aggregate per-file user additions for accurate modification tracking
   178→	var accumulatedUserAdded, accumulatedUserRemoved int
   179→	accumulatedUserAddedPerFile := make(map[string]int)
   180→	for _, pa := range promptAttributions {
   181→		accumulatedUserAdded += pa.UserLinesAdded
   182→		accumulatedUserRemoved += pa.UserLinesRemoved
   183→		// Merge per-file data from all prompt attributions
   184→		for filePath, added := range pa.UserAddedPerFile {
   185→			accumulatedUserAddedPerFile[filePath] += added
   186→		}
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	// Calculate attribution for agent-touched files
   190→	// IMPORTANT: shadowTree is a snapshot of the worktree at checkpoint time,
   191→	// which includes both agent work AND accumulated user edits (to agent-touched files).
   192→	// So base→shadow diff = (agent work + accumulated user work to these files).
   193→	var totalAgentAndUserWork int
   194→	var postCheckpointUserAdded, postCheckpointUserRemoved int
   195→	postCheckpointUserRemovedPerFile := make(map[string]int)
   196→
   197→	for _, filePath := range filesTouched {
   198→		baseContent := getFileContent(baseTree, filePath)
   199→		shadowContent := getFileContent(shadowTree, filePath)
   200→		headContent := getFileContent(headTree, filePath)
   201→
   202→		// Total work in shadow: base → shadow (agent + accumulated user work for this file)
   203→		_, workAdded, _ := diffLines(baseContent, shadowContent)
   204→		totalAgentAndUserWork += workAdded
   205→
   206→		// Post-checkpoint user edits: shadow → head (only post-checkpoint edits for this file)
   207→		_, postUserAdded, postUserRemoved := diffLines(shadowContent, headContent)
   208→		postCheckpointUserAdded += postUserAdded
   209→		postCheckpointUserRemoved += postUserRemoved
   210→
   211→		// Track per-file removals for self-modification estimation
   212→		if postUserRemoved > 0 {
   213→			postCheckpointUserRemovedPerFile[filePath] = postUserRemoved
   214→		}
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	// Calculate total user edits to non-agent files (files not in filesTouched)
   218→	// These files are not in the shadow tree, so base→head captures ALL their user edits
   219→	nonAgentFiles := getAllChangedFilesBetweenTrees(baseTree, headTree)
   220→	var allUserEditsToNonAgentFiles int
   221→	for _, filePath := range nonAgentFiles {
   222→		if slices.Contains(filesTouched, filePath) {
   223→			continue // Skip agent-touched files
   224→		}
   225→
   226→		baseContent := getFileContent(baseTree, filePath)
   227→		headContent := getFileContent(headTree, filePath)
   228→		_, userAdded, _ := diffLines(baseContent, headContent)
   229→		allUserEditsToNonAgentFiles += userAdded
   230→	}
   231→
   232→	// Separate accumulated edits by file type using per-file tracking data.
   233→	// This is precise because accumulatedUserAddedPerFile tells us exactly which files
   234→	// the user edited between checkpoints.
   235→	var accumulatedToAgentFiles, accumulatedToNonAgentFiles int
   236→	for filePath, added := range accumulatedUserAddedPerFile {
   237→		if slices.Contains(filesTouched, filePath) {
   238→			accumulatedToAgentFiles += added
   239→		} else {
   240→			accumulatedToNonAgentFiles += added
   241→		}
   242→	}
   243→
   244→	// Agent work = (base→shadow for agent files) - (accumulated user edits to agent files only)
   245→	totalAgentAdded := max(0, totalAgentAndUserWork-accumulatedToAgentFiles)
   246→
   247→	// Post-checkpoint edits to non-agent files = total edits - accumulated portion (never negative)
   248→	postToNonAgentFiles := max(0, allUserEditsToNonAgentFiles-accumulatedToNonAgentFiles)
   249→
   250→	// Total user contribution = accumulated (all files) + post-checkpoint (agent files) + post-checkpoint (non-agent files)
   251→	totalUserAdded := accumulatedUserAdded + postCheckpointUserAdded + postToNonAgentFiles
   252→	totalUserRemoved := accumulatedUserRemoved + postCheckpointUserRemoved
   253→
   254→	// Estimate modified lines (user changed existing lines)
   255→	// Lines that were both added and removed are treated as modifications.
   256→	totalHumanModified := min(totalUserAdded, totalUserRemoved)
   257→
   258→	// Estimate user self-modifications using per-file tracking (see docs/architecture/attribution.md)
   259→	// When a user removes lines from a file, assume they're removing their own lines first (LIFO).
   260→	// Only after exhausting their own additions should we count removals as targeting agent lines.
   261→	userSelfModified := estimateUserSelfModifications(accumulatedUserAddedPerFile, postCheckpointUserRemovedPerFile)
   262→
   263→	// humanModifiedAgent = modifications that targeted agent lines (not user's own lines)
   264→	humanModifiedAgent := max(0, totalHumanModified-userSelfModified)
   265→
   266→	// Remaining modifications are user self-modifications (user edited their own code)
   267→	// These should NOT be subtracted from agent lines
   268→	pureUserAdded := totalUserAdded - totalHumanModified
   269→	pureUserRemoved := totalUserRemoved - totalHumanModified
   270→
   271→	// Total net additions = agent additions + pure user additions - pure user removals
   272→	// This reconstructs the base → head diff from our tracked changes.
   273→	// Note: This measures "net new lines added to the codebase" not total file size.
   274→	// pureUserRemoved represents agent lines that the user deleted, so we subtract them.
   275→	totalCommitted := totalAgentAdded + pureUserAdded - pureUserRemoved
   276→	if totalCommitted <= 0 {
   277→		// Fallback for delete-only commits or when removals exceed additions
   278→		// Note: If both are 0 (deletion-only commit where agent added nothing),
   279→		// totalCommitted will be 0 and percentage will be 0. This is expected -
   280→		// the attribution percentage is only meaningful for commits that add code.
   281→		totalCommitted = max(0, totalAgentAdded)
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	// Calculate agent lines actually in the commit (excluding removed and modified)
   285→	// Agent added lines, but user removed some and modified others.
   286→	// Only subtract modifications that targeted AGENT lines (humanModifiedAgent),
   287→	// not user self-modifications.
   288→	// Clamp to 0 to handle cases where user removed/modified more than agent added.
   289→	agentLinesInCommit := max(0, totalAgentAdded-pureUserRemoved-humanModifiedAgent)
   290→
   291→	// Calculate percentage
   292→	var agentPercentage float64
   293→	if totalCommitted > 0 {
   294→		agentPercentage = float64(agentLinesInCommit) / float64(totalCommitted) * 100
   295→	}
   296→
   297→	return &checkpoint.InitialAttribution{
   298→		CalculatedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
   299→		AgentLines:      agentLinesInCommit,
   300→		HumanAdded:      pureUserAdded,
   301→		HumanModified:   totalHumanModified, // Total modifications (for reporting)
   302→		HumanRemoved:    pureUserRemoved,
   303→		TotalCommitted:  totalCommitted,
   304→		AgentPercentage: agentPercentage,
   305→	}
   306→}
   307→
   308→// estimateUserSelfModifications estimates how many removed lines were the user's own additions.
   309→// Uses LIFO assumption: when a user removes lines from a file, they likely remove their own
   310→// recent additions before touching agent lines.
   311→//
   312→// See docs/architecture/attribution.md for the rationale behind this heuristic.
   313→func estimateUserSelfModifications(
   314→	accumulatedUserAddedPerFile map[string]int,
   315→	postCheckpointUserRemovedPerFile map[string]int,
   316→) int {
   317→	var selfModified int
   318→	for filePath, removed := range postCheckpointUserRemovedPerFile {
   319→		userAddedToFile := accumulatedUserAddedPerFile[filePath]
   320→		// User can only self-modify up to what they previously added
   321→		selfModified += min(removed, userAddedToFile)
   322→	}
   323→	return selfModified
   324→}
   325→
   326→// CalculatePromptAttribution computes line-level attribution at the start of a prompt.
   327→// This captures user edits since the last checkpoint BEFORE the agent makes changes.
   328→//
   329→// Parameters:
   330→//   - baseTree: the tree at session start (the base commit)
   331→//   - lastCheckpointTree: the tree from the previous checkpoint (nil if first checkpoint)
   332→//   - worktreeFiles: map of file path → current worktree content for files that changed
   333→//   - checkpointNumber: which checkpoint we're about to create (1-indexed)
   334→//
   335→// Returns the attribution data to store in session state. For checkpoint 1 (when
   336→// lastCheckpointTree is nil), AgentLinesAdded/Removed will be 0 since there's no
   337→// previous checkpoint to measure cumulative agent work against.
   338→//
   339→// Note: Binary files (detected by null bytes) in reference trees are silently excluded
   340→// from attribution calculations since line-based diffing only applies to text files.
   341→func CalculatePromptAttribution(
   342→	baseTree *object.Tree,
   343→	lastCheckpointTree *object.Tree,
   344→	worktreeFiles map[string]string,
   345→	checkpointNumber int,
   346→) PromptAttribution {
   347→	result := PromptAttribution{
   348→		CheckpointNumber: checkpointNumber,
   349→		UserAddedPerFile: make(map[string]int),
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	if len(worktreeFiles) == 0 {
   353→		return result
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	// Determine reference tree for user changes (last checkpoint or base)
   357→	referenceTree := lastCheckpointTree
   358→	if referenceTree == nil {
   359→		referenceTree = baseTree
   360→	}
   361→
   362→	for filePath, worktreeContent := range worktreeFiles {
   363→		referenceContent := getFileContent(referenceTree, filePath)
   364→		baseContent := getFileContent(baseTree, filePath)
   365→
   366→		// User changes: diff(reference, worktree)
   367→		// These are changes since the last checkpoint that the agent didn't make
   368→		_, userAdded, userRemoved := diffLines(referenceContent, worktreeContent)
   369→		result.UserLinesAdded += userAdded
   370→		result.UserLinesRemoved += userRemoved
   371→
   372→		// Track per-file user additions for accurate modification tracking.
   373→		// This enables distinguishing user self-modifications from agent modifications.
   374→		if userAdded > 0 {
   375→			result.UserAddedPerFile[filePath] = userAdded
   376→		}
   377→
   378→		// Agent lines so far: diff(base, lastCheckpoint)
   379→		// Only calculate if we have a previous checkpoint
   380→		if lastCheckpointTree != nil {
   381→			checkpointContent := getFileContent(lastCheckpointTree, filePath)
   382→			_, agentAdded, agentRemoved := diffLines(baseContent, checkpointContent)
   383→			result.AgentLinesAdded += agentAdded
   384→			result.AgentLinesRemoved += agentRemoved
   385→		}
   386→	}
   387→
   388→	return result
   389→}
   390→

    31→// hasTTY checks if /dev/tty is available for interactive prompts.
    32→// Returns false when running as an agent subprocess (no controlling terminal).
    33→//
    34→// In test environments, ENTIRE_TEST_TTY overrides the real check:
    35→//   - ENTIRE_TEST_TTY=1 → simulate human (TTY available)
    36→//   - ENTIRE_TEST_TTY=0 → simulate agent (no TTY)
    37→func hasTTY() bool {
    38→	if v := os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TEST_TTY"); v != "" {
    39→		return v == "1"
    40→	}
    41→	tty, err := os.OpenFile("/dev/tty", os.O_RDWR, 0)
    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		return false
    44→	}
    45→	_ = tty.Close()
    46→	return true
    47→}
    48→
    49→// askConfirmTTY prompts the user for a yes/no confirmation via /dev/tty.
    50→// This works even when stdin is redirected (e.g., git commit -m).
    51→// Returns true for yes, false for no. If TTY is unavailable, returns the default.
    52→// If context is non-empty, it is displayed on a separate line before the prompt.
    53→func askConfirmTTY(prompt string, context string, defaultYes bool) bool {
    54→	// In test mode, don't try to interact with the real TTY — just use the default.
    55→	// ENTIRE_TEST_TTY=1 simulates "a human is present" for the hasTTY() check
    56→	// but we can't actually read from the TTY in tests.
    57→	if os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TEST_TTY") != "" {
    58→		return defaultYes
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	// Open /dev/tty for both reading and writing
    62→	// This is the controlling terminal, which works even when stdin/stderr are redirected
    63→	tty, err := os.OpenFile("/dev/tty", os.O_RDWR, 0)
    64→	if err != nil {
    65→		// Can't open TTY (e.g., running in CI), use default
    66→		return defaultYes
    67→	}
    68→	defer tty.Close()
    69→
    70→	// Show context if provided
    71→	if context != "" {
    72→		fmt.Fprintf(tty, "%s\n", context)
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Show prompt with default indicator
    76→	// Write to tty directly, not stderr, since git hooks may redirect stderr to /dev/null
    77→	var hint string
    78→	if defaultYes {
    79→		hint = "[Y/n]"
    80→	} else {
    81→		hint = "[y/N]"
    82→	}
    83→	fmt.Fprintf(tty, "%s %s ", prompt, hint)
    84→
    85→	// Read response
    86→	reader := bufio.NewReader(tty)
    87→	response, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
    88→	if err != nil {
    89→		return defaultYes
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	response = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(response))
    93→	switch response {

    95→		return true
    96→	case "n", "no":
    97→		return false
    98→	default:
    99→		// Empty or invalid input - use default
   100→		return defaultYes
   101→	}
   102→}
   103→
   104→// CommitMsg is called by the git commit-msg hook after the user edits the message.
   105→// If the message contains only our trailer (no actual user content), strip it
   106→// so git will abort the commit due to empty message.
   107→//
   108→//nolint:unparam // error return required by interface but hooks must return nil
   109→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) CommitMsg(commitMsgFile string) error {
   110→	content, err := os.ReadFile(commitMsgFile) //nolint:gosec // Path comes from git hook
   111→	if err != nil {
   112→		return nil //nolint:nilerr // Hook must be silent on failure
   113→	}
   114→
   115→	message := string(content)
   116→
   117→	// Check if our trailer is present (ParseCheckpoint validates format, so found==true means valid)
   118→	if _, found := trailers.ParseCheckpoint(message); !found {
   119→		// No trailer, nothing to do
   120→		return nil
   121→	}
   122→
   123→	// Check if there's any user content (non-comment, non-trailer lines)
   124→	if !hasUserContent(message) {
   125→		// No user content - strip the trailer so git aborts
   126→		message = stripCheckpointTrailer(message)
   127→		if err := os.WriteFile(commitMsgFile, []byte(message), 0o600); err != nil {
   128→			return nil //nolint:nilerr // Hook must be silent on failure
   129→		}
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	return nil
   133→}
   134→
   135→// hasUserContent checks if the message has any content besides comments and our trailer.
   136→func hasUserContent(message string) bool {
   137→	trailerPrefix := trailers.CheckpointTrailerKey + ":"
   138→	for _, line := range strings.Split(message, "\n") {
   139→		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
   140→		// Skip empty lines
   141→		if trimmed == "" {
   142→			continue
   143→		}
   144→		// Skip comment lines
   145→		if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "#") {
   146→			continue
   147→		}
   148→		// Skip our trailer line
   149→		if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, trailerPrefix) {
   150→			continue
   151→		}
   152→		// Found user content
   153→		return true
   154→	}
   155→	return false
   156→}
   157→
   158→// stripCheckpointTrailer removes the Entire-Checkpoint trailer line from the message.
   159→func stripCheckpointTrailer(message string) string {
   160→	trailerPrefix := trailers.CheckpointTrailerKey + ":"
   161→	var result []string
   162→	for _, line := range strings.Split(message, "\n") {
   163→		if !strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(line), trailerPrefix) {
   164→			result = append(result, line)
   165→		}
   166→	}
   167→	return strings.Join(result, "\n")
   168→}
